description: Test your knowledge of bone and joint anatomy.

cards.text: What are the three main regions of a long bone?

tip.tip: Think shaft, ends, and transition zone

cards.text: Name the three sources of blood supply to a bone.
cards.answer: Nutrient artery, periosteal arteries, epiphyseal and metaphyseal arteries

tip.tip: One enters the shaft, others supply the surface and ends

cards.text: What is the largest sesamoid bone in the body?

tip.tip: Found in the tendon of the quadriceps

cards.text: Name the three types of cartilage.

tip.tip: Most common type covers articular surfaces

cards.text: What type of bone has two plates of compact bone with diploe between them?

tip.tip: Examples include skull vault, scapula, sternum

cards.text: Which bone is unique for being a long bone that ossifies in membrane?

tip.tip: It also has no medullary cavity

cards.text: What law states that a nerve supplying a joint also supplies the muscles moving it and the overlying skin?

tip.tip: Named after the English surgeon John Hilton

cards.text: What is the name for bones that develop within tendons?

tip.tip: The patella and pisiform are examples

cards.text: What type of epiphysis is related to muscle pull rather than joint formation?

tip.tip: Osgood-Schlatter disease affects this type at the tibial tuberosity

cards.text: In the classification of joints, what type has a joint cavity lined by synovial membrane?
